atoms.paper_title: Realizing Scaling Laws in Recommender Systems: A Foundation-Expert Paradigm for Hyperscale Model Deployment
atoms.text: Foundation-Expert Paradigm, a framework designed for the development and deployment of hyperscale recommendation foundation models, deployed at Meta serving tens of billions of user requests daily
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Foundation-Expert Paradigm, a framework designed for the development and deployment of hyperscale recommendation foundation models, deployed at Meta serving tens of billions of user requests daily.

atoms.paper_title: Will LLMs Scaling Hit the Wall? Breaking Barriers via Distributed Resources on Massive Edge Devices
atoms.text: Reviews recent technical advancements in distributed/federated learning that make a new paradigm viable, revealing the vast untapped potential of data and computational resources on massive edge devices
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Reviews recent technical advancements in distributed/federated learning that make a new paradigm viable, revealing the vast untapped potential of data and computational resources on massive edge devices.

atoms.paper_title: The Race to Efficiency: A New Perspective on AI Scaling Laws
atoms.text: Presents a time- and efficiency-aware foundation for AI scaling, revealing how a race to efficiency naturally emerges once classical, static scaling approaches are considered
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Presents a time- and efficiency-aware foundation for AI scaling, revealing how a race to efficiency naturally emerges once classical, static scaling approaches are considered.

atoms.paper_title: DirMoE: Dirichlet-routed Mixture of Experts
atoms.text: Introduces a Dirichlet variational router that factorizes routing into a binary selection vector over experts and a Dirichlet distribution over mixture weights, producing a relaxed expert selection vector via Gumbel-Sigmoid reparameterization
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Introduces a Dirichlet variational router that factorizes routing into a binary selection vector over experts and a Dirichlet distribution over mixture weights, producing a relaxed expert selection vector via Gumbel-Sigmoid reparameterization.

atoms.paper_title: Input Domain Aware MoE: Decoupling Routing Decisions from Task Optimization in Mixture of Experts
atoms.text: Proposes a novel routing framework that leverages a probabilistic mixture model to better partition the input space, with routing mechanism trained independently of task-specific objectives for stable optimization
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Proposes a novel routing framework that leverages a probabilistic mixture model to better partition the input space, with routing mechanism trained independently of task-specific objectives for stable optimization.

atoms.paper_title: Mixture-of-Experts with Gradient Conflict-Driven Subspace Topology Pruning for Emergent Modularity
atoms.text: Rather than treating gradient conflict as optimization noise to be suppressed, they leverage it as a structural supervisory signal by monitoring the cosine similarity of gradients between experts on the shared backbone to penalize connections that cause interference
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Rather than treating gradient conflict as optimization noise to be suppressed, they leverage it as a structural supervisory signal by monitoring the cosine similarity of gradients between experts on the shared backbone to penalize connections that cause interference.

atoms.paper_title: Grounding Long-Context Reasoning with Contextual Normalization for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
atoms.text: addresses how mechanisms for resolving conflicts between retrieved evidence and the model's parametric memory remain unclear
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Grounding long-context reasoning with contextual normalization for retrieval-augmented generation; addresses how mechanisms for resolving conflicts between retrieved evidence and the model's parametric memory remain unclear.

atoms.paper_title: Resisting Contextual Interference in RAG via Parametric-Knowledge Reinforcement (Knowledgeable-R1)
atoms.text: Employs asymmetric advantage transformation amplifying exploratory behaviors toward parametric knowledge, outperforming baselines by +22.89% in counterfactual scenarios
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Employs asymmetric advantage transformation amplifying exploratory behaviors toward parametric knowledge, outperforming baselines by +22.89% in counterfactual scenarios.

atoms.paper_title: When Retrieval Succeeds and Fails: Rethinking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for LLMs
atoms.text: Mechanisms for resolving conflicts between retrieved evidence and the model's parametric memory remain unclear, often leading to unpredictable behavior
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Mechanisms for resolving conflicts between retrieved evidence and the model's parametric memory remain unclear, often leading to unpredictable behavior.

atoms.paper_title: TPS-Bench: Evaluating AI Agents' Tool Planning & Scheduling Abilities in Compounding Tasks
atoms.text: Real-world scenarios often involve compounding tasks that demand the collaboration of various tools
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Real-world scenarios often involve compounding tasks that demand the collaboration of various tools; most models exhibit reasonable performance in tool planning but behave differently in scheduling.

atoms.paper_title: UltraHorizon: Benchmarking Agent Capabilities in Ultra Long-Horizon Scenarios
atoms.text: LLM-agents consistently underperform while human participants achieve higher scores
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Trajectories averaging 200k+ tokens with 400+ tool calls; LLM-agents consistently underperform while human participants achieve higher scores. Eight error types attributed to in-context locking and fundamental capability gaps; meltdown behavior emerges in long-horizon trajectories.

atoms.paper_title: Kosmos: An AI Scientist for Autonomous Discovery
atoms.text: Given an open-ended objective and a dataset, Kosmos runs for up to 12 hours performing cycles of parallel data analysis, literature search, and hypothesis generation before synthesizing discoveries into scientific reports
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Given an open-ended objective and a dataset, Kosmos runs for up to 12 hours performing cycles of parallel data analysis, literature search, and hypothesis generation before synthesizing discoveries into scientific reports.

atoms.paper_title: V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning
atoms.text: Pre-trained action-free joint-embedding-predictive architecture trained on a video and image dataset comprising over 1 million hours of internet video
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Pre-trained action-free joint-embedding-predictive architecture trained on a video and image dataset comprising over 1 million hours of internet video; deployed zero-shot on Franka arms in two different labs for picking and placing with image goals.

atoms.paper_title: VL-JEPA: Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for Vision-language
atoms.text: Joint embedding predictive architecture for vision-language extends JEPA framework to multimodal inputs by predicting latent representations rather than pixel reconstruction
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Joint embedding predictive architecture for vision-language extends JEPA framework to multimodal inputs by predicting latent representations rather than pixel reconstruction.

atoms.paper_title: VLA-JEPA: Enhancing Vision-Language-Action Model with Latent World Model
atoms.text: Enhances vision-language-action model with a latent world model derived from JEPA predictive embeddings to improve sample efficiency of robot policy learning
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Enhances vision-language-action model with a latent world model derived from JEPA predictive embeddings to improve sample efficiency of robot policy learning.

atoms.paper_title: Test-Time Training Provably Improves Transformers as In-context Learners
atoms.text: Test-time training methods explicitly update model weights to adapt to specific test instances
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Test-time training methods explicitly update model weights to adapt to specific test instances; theoretical characterization of linear transformers with single gradient step updates examines distribution alignment and how TTT alleviates distribution shift.

atoms.paper_title: Test time training enhances in-context learning of nonlinear functions
atoms.text: integrating TTT with in-context learning by using in-context examples as adaptation data achieves improvements on few-shot reasoning benchmarks
atoms.verbatim_snippet: TTT adapts models by updating parameters on test data before each prediction; integrating TTT with in-context learning by using in-context examples as adaptation data achieves improvements on few-shot reasoning benchmarks.

atoms.paper_title: Towards Theoretical Understanding of Transformer Test-Time
atoms.text: Addresses the gap in theoretical understanding of test-time computation for transformer in-context learning, characterizing sample complexity benefit proportional to the number of target examples within the prompt
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Addresses the gap in theoretical understanding of test-time computation for transformer in-context learning, characterizing sample complexity benefit proportional to the number of target examples within the prompt.

atoms.paper_title: Reward Shaping to Mitigate Reward Hacking in RLHF
atoms.text: RLHF is susceptible to reward hacking where the agent exploits flaws in the reward function rather than learning the intended behavior
atoms.verbatim_snippet: RLHF is susceptible to reward hacking where the agent exploits flaws in the reward function rather than learning the intended behavior; identifies two design principles: the RL reward should be bounded, and should have rapid initial growth followed by gradual convergence.

atoms.paper_title: Reward Hacking Mitigation using Verifiable Composite Rewards (RLVR)
atoms.text: Leverages the existing RLHF objective but replaces the reward model with a verification function
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Leverages the existing RLHF objective but replaces the reward model with a verification function; the policy only receives a reward when its generated responses are verifiably correct.

atoms.paper_title: Mitigating Reward Hacking in RLHF via Advantage Sign Robustness
atoms.text: Proposes that reward hacking is often caused by flipped advantage signs, derives a certified sign-preservation radius which is the smallest perturbation that can flip the advantage sign during policy optimization
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Proposes that reward hacking is often caused by flipped advantage signs, derives a certified sign-preservation radius which is the smallest perturbation that can flip the advantage sign during policy optimization.

atoms.paper_title: Mechanistic Interpretability with Sparse Autoencoder Neural Operators
atoms.text: SAEs are employed in mechanistic interpretability to decompose the hidden activations of large language models into sparse latent representations that ideally correspond to semantically distinct, causal features
atoms.verbatim_snippet: SAEs are employed in mechanistic interpretability to decompose the hidden activations of large language models into sparse latent representations that ideally correspond to semantically distinct, causal features.

atoms.paper_title: Resurrecting the Salmon: Rethinking Mechanistic Interpretability with Domain-Specific Sparse Autoencoders
atoms.text: Domain-specific sparse autoencoders rethink mechanistic interpretability
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Domain-specific sparse autoencoders rethink mechanistic interpretability; circuits defined as subgraphs of a model's computation graph that implement a task-specific behaviour.

atoms.paper_title: Sparse Feature Circuits: Discovering and Editing Interpretable Causal Graphs in Language Models
atoms.text: Discovers and edits interpretable causal graphs in language models by composing SAE-extracted features into circuits that can be ablated or amplified
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Discovers and edits interpretable causal graphs in language models by composing SAE-extracted features into circuits that can be ablated or amplified.

atoms.paper_title: Evidence of Phase Transitions in Small Transformer-Based Language Models
atoms.text: Phase transitions proposed as the origin of emergent abilities in large language models
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Phase transitions proposed as the origin of emergent abilities in large language models; new capabilities appear abruptly once models surpass critical thresholds of scale.

atoms.paper_title: Why are LLMs' abilities emergent?
atoms.text: debate remains about whether jumps are discontinuous or continuous under different metrics
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Emergent abilities arise from the complex dynamics of highly sensitive nonlinear systems rather than simply from parameter scaling; debate remains about whether jumps are discontinuous or continuous under different metrics.

atoms.paper_title: Large Language Models and Emergence: A Complex Systems Perspective
atoms.text: Argues emergent abilities arise from complex-systems dynamics
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Argues emergent abilities arise from complex-systems dynamics; covariance spectra of weights transition from exponential to scale-free concomitantly with the peak of double-descent behavior.

atoms.paper_title: AlphaResearch: Accelerating New Algorithm Discovery with Language Models
atoms.text: achieves stronger discovery performance than other agentic discovery systems on six open-ended problems
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Autonomous research agent that synergistically combines new idea generation with program-based verification for novel algorithm discovery; achieves stronger discovery performance than other agentic discovery systems on six open-ended problems.

atoms.paper_title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
atoms.text: First framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution on open-ended problems
atoms.verbatim_snippet: First framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution on open-ended problems; achieves 3-10x higher improvement rates with far fewer evaluations than fixed evolutionary search baselines on 10 tasks.

atoms.paper_title: AutoDiscovery: Open-ended Scientific Discovery via Bayesian Surprise
atoms.text: Finds 5-29% more hypotheses surprising to the LLM agent compared to strong search baselines
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Finds 5-29% more hypotheses surprising to the LLM agent compared to strong search baselines; two-thirds of surprising discoveries correlate with human surprisal in a human study.

atoms.paper_title: Beyond Pipelines: A Survey of the Paradigm Shift toward Model-Native Agentic AI
atoms.text: An emerging model-native paradigm is shifting from the traditional pipeline-based approach where agents were composite systems linked through prompts, toward a unified model that through end-to-end training learns to autonomously perform high-level functions
atoms.verbatim_snippet: An emerging model-native paradigm is shifting from the traditional pipeline-based approach where agents were composite systems linked through prompts, toward a unified model that through end-to-end training learns to autonomously perform high-level functions.

atoms.paper_title: Humanity in the Age of AI: Reassessing 2025's Existential-Risk Narratives
atoms.text: Despite 60 years of speculation, phenomena like sustained recursive self-improvement and autonomous strategic awareness have not been observed
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Despite 60 years of speculation, phenomena like sustained recursive self-improvement and autonomous strategic awareness have not been observed; current generative models remain narrow statistically trained artifacts lacking the properties that would make catastrophic scenarios plausible.

atoms.paper_title: Improving the Efficiency of LLM Agent Systems through Trajectory Reduction
atoms.text: Focuses on the efficiency concern of coding LLM agents, which is neglected by existing studies
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Focuses on the efficiency concern of coding LLM agents, which is neglected by existing studies; proposes trajectory reduction to compress repeated execution states.

atoms.paper_title: Position: LLM Serving Needs Mathematical Optimization and Algorithmic Foundations, Not Just Heuristics
atoms.text: Classical scheduling questions complicated by LLM-specific structure of growing memory consumption and unknown job durations, suggest opportunities for principled scheduling algorithms
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Classical scheduling questions complicated by LLM-specific structure of growing memory consumption and unknown job durations, suggest opportunities for principled scheduling algorithms; argues current LLM serving relies on heuristics rather than algorithmic foundations.

atoms.paper_title: A Survey of Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models under Data Scarcity: Challenges and Solutions
atoms.text: Reinforcement learning for large language models under data scarcity raises distinct challenges in sample efficiency, off-policy correction, and exploration that are not addressed by standard online RLHF pipelines
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Reinforcement learning for large language models under data scarcity raises distinct challenges in sample efficiency, off-policy correction, and exploration that are not addressed by standard online RLHF pipelines.

atoms.paper_title: Grounded in Reality: Learning and Deploying Proactive LLM from Offline Logs
atoms.text: Learning proactive LLM behavior from offline interaction logs rather than online preference labeling enables grounding in deployed-system reality without active human-in-the-loop, an under-explored alternative to online RLHF
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Learning proactive LLM behavior from offline interaction logs rather than online preference labeling enables grounding in deployed-system reality without active human-in-the-loop, an under-explored alternative to online RLHF.

atoms.paper_title: Evolutionary Developmental Biology and AI Paradigm
atoms.text: introduces a theoretical framework that evolutionary developmental biology offers for a new design paradigm for AI
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Moves beyond superficial biological analogies and instead grounds itself in fundamental conceptual principles of adaptation and biological organization; introduces a theoretical framework that evolutionary developmental biology offers for a new design paradigm for AI.

atoms.paper_title: NeuroAI and Beyond: Bridging Between Advances in Neuroscience and Artificial Intelligence
atoms.text: Three fundamental capability gaps in current AI were identified: inability to interact with the physical world, inadequate learning that produces brittle systems, and unsustainable energy and data inefficiency
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Three fundamental capability gaps in current AI were identified: inability to interact with the physical world, inadequate learning that produces brittle systems, and unsustainable energy and data inefficiency; neuroscience principles address each including co-design of body and controller, prediction through interaction, and sparse event-driven computation.

atoms.paper_title: Deep Learning of Thermodynamic Laws from Microscopic Dynamics
atoms.text: the work suggests that DNNs perform a form of statistical mechanics and raises the possibility of a novel statistical mechanical framework for understanding learning itself
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Deep neural networks can learn thermodynamic principles from microscopic dynamics; the work suggests that DNNs perform a form of statistical mechanics and raises the possibility of a novel statistical mechanical framework for understanding learning itself.

atoms.paper_title: From Competition to Coordination: Market Making as a Scalable Framework for Safe and Aligned Multi-Agent LLM Systems
atoms.text: Market-based coordination yields accuracy gains of up to 10% over single-shot baselines while preserving interpretability and transparency
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Market-based coordination yields accuracy gains of up to 10% over single-shot baselines while preserving interpretability and transparency; economic coordination principles can operationalize accountability in multi-agent LLM systems.

atoms.paper_title: Thinking Beyond Tokens: From Brain-Inspired Intelligence to Cognitive Foundations for AGI
atoms.text: Offers a cross-disciplinary synthesis of AGI development spanning artificial intelligence, cognitive neuroscience, psychology, generative models, and agent-based systems
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Offers a cross-disciplinary synthesis of AGI development spanning artificial intelligence, cognitive neuroscience, psychology, generative models, and agent-based systems.

atoms.paper_title: LLM Economist: Large Population Models and Mechanism Design in Multi-Agent Generative Simulacra
atoms.text: Introduces a fully language-based framework that embeds a population of persona-conditioned agents and a tax planner in a two-tier Stackelberg game for mechanism design
atoms.verbatim_snippet: Introduces a fully language-based framework that embeds a population of persona-conditioned agents and a tax planner in a two-tier Stackelberg game for mechanism design.
